pi_items.source: OpenSIS documentation confirms 'Half Day' as a standard attendance code option in the system
Standard abbreviation for Half Day in attendance systems; task setup script queries for short_name='HD'
Task setup and export scripts reference attendance code types; 'Official' is a valid OpenSIS code type

Task export_result.sh queries database for state_code field; however, verified state code standards (Alabama attendance codes) only recognize 'Half' not 'HD' as a state code. OpenSIS allows custom state codes, so 'HD' is task-specific.
OpenSIS documentation mentions support for virtual schools and attendance tracking; 'Virtual Attendance' is a plausible custom code for virtual school contexts
Standard abbreviation for Virtual Attendance; task setup script queries for short_name='VA'
Task setup and export scripts reference attendance code types; 'Official' is a valid OpenSIS code type
Task export_result.sh queries database for state_code field; 'VA' is not in standard state code lists but is a custom code created for this task
Setup script uses 'admin' as test username for OpenSIS test environment; credentials are embedded in setup_task.sh
Setup script uses 'Admin@123' as test password for OpenSIS test environment; credentials are embedded in setup_task.sh

privileged_info_summary: The task configures two attendance codes in OpenSIS v9.2: 'Half Day' (HD) and 'Virtual Attendance' (VA), both marked as Official type codes. 'Half Day' is a standard OpenSIS attendance code option. The state codes HD and VA are custom codes not found in standard state attendance code taxonomies (e.g., Alabama official codes), but OpenSIS allows schools to define custom state codes. All data is synthetic, created in a test OpenSIS instance running on MariaDB. Test credentials (admin/Admin@123) are for the test environment only. The task verifies success by querying the attendance_codes table for records matching short_name='HD' and short_name='VA' with correct attributes (title, type, state_code fields).
